title: Drive - Home to Rapid City
address: 1416 N Elk Vale Rd, Rapid City, SD 57703

title: Drive - Rapid City to Devils Tower
Stop for fishing gear on the way
description: Stop in Cody to grab fishing gear before heading to Yellowstone. Hour less if we did Devils Tower.

title: Drive to northeast Yellowstone entrance
address: 212, Northeast Entrance, US-212, Cooke City, MT 59081

title: Trout Lake Trail & Fishing
description: 🥾 Short Hike & Lure Fishing: 1.2 mi loop, easy. A peaceful alpine lake hike with frequent river otter and osprey sightings. Trout Lake is a premier spot for spin-fishing with lead-free, barbless lures. Target large native cutthroat trout (must release unharmed) and rainbow trout from the shore. NOTE: The inlet stream and cove are permanently closed to fishing to protect spawning trout.

title: Lamar Valley Wildlife Viewing
description: 🦬 Wildlife: The crown jewel of Yellowstone wildlife viewing. Bison herds, wolves, grizzly bears, and pronghorn are all commonly spotted. Pull off at any turnout and scan the hillsides with binoculars — early afternoon is still productive but dawn/dusk are peak times.

title: Calcite Springs Overlook & Petrified Tree
address: Yellowstone National Park, Grand Loop Rd, Mammoth, WY 82190
description: 🌋 Geologic Features: Calcite Springs Overlook offers dramatic views of columnar basalt cliffs dropping into the Yellowstone River canyon. Just up the road, the Petrified Tree is a lone ancient redwood turned to stone by volcanic ash millions of years ago — a quick roadside stop.

title: Wraith Falls
address: Yellowstone National Park, Grand Loop Rd, Mammoth, WY 82190
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 0.8 mi out-and-back, easy. A beautiful 100-ft cascade tucked into a meadow near Mammoth. One of the most rewarding short hikes in the northern range — easy enough for the whole family.

title: Mammoth Hot Springs Terraces
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: A surreal landscape of travertine terraces built by calcium carbonate-rich water. Walk the Lower Terrace Loop (1.5 mi, easy boardwalk) to explore both active steaming terraces and pale dormant formations. One of the most otherworldly sights in the park.

title: Tower Fall
address: 1 Dunraven Pass Road, Yellowstone National Park, WY 82190
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: A dramatic 132-ft waterfall carved through volcanic rock. Park at the general store and then a short walk to the overlook. The basalt columns framing the falls are a great example of the park's volcanic geology.

title: Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: Marvel at the breathtaking canyon walls and the spectacular 308-ft Lower Falls from Artist Point and other iconic rim overlooks. The brilliant yellow, pink, and orange canyon walls are a dramatic showcase of the park's volcanic history. Dirt in my Shoes recommends North Rim Scenic Drive, Brink of the Lower Falls, Lookout Point, Red Rock Point Trail (0.7 mi), and Artist Point.

title: Hayden Valley Wildlife Viewing
description: 🦬 Wildlife: Prime bison and grizzly bear territory on the drive toward West Yellowstone. Slow down and scan the open meadows — this broad glacially-carved valley is one of the best places in the park to spot large mammals. Pelican Valley and the Yellowstone Lake shoreline nearby are excellent for waterfowl and trumpeter swans.

title: Mud Volcano Trail
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 0.7 mi RT. Dirt on my Shoes recommends Dragon's Mouth and Mud Volcano.

title: Storm Point Trail
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 2.6 mi loop, easy. Hugs the Yellowstone Lake shoreline with frequent bison and waterfowl sightings. Check the trailhead for bear activity before heading out — this area sees regular grizzly use. Great views across the lake toward the Absaroka Range.

title: Natural Bridge Trail
address: Natural Brg Trl, Yellowstone National Park, WY 82190
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 3.1 mi out-and-back, easy. Leads to a 51-ft rhyolite arch carved by Bridge Creek — one of the park's lesser-known geological gems. A quiet trail away from the crowds near Bridge Bay.

title: Yellowstone Lake & Grant Village Wildlife
address: 2 Grant Village Rd, Yellowstone National Park, WY 82190
description: 🦬 Wildlife: The meadows near Grant Village often have bison and elk in the early morning and evening. Yellowstone Lake is a hotspot for osprey, bald eagles, and river otters along the shoreline. Keep an eye out for black bears along the forest edges on the south entrance road as you head toward Jackson.

title: West Thumb Geyser Basin
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: A unique spot where hydrothermal features meet the shore of Yellowstone Lake. Walk the short boardwalk loop past boiling pools and the famous Fishing Cone — a submerged geyser where early visitors would cook fish directly in the boiling water.

title: Old Faithful & Upper Geyser Basin
description: ⛲ Geysers: Catch an Old Faithful eruption (roughly every 90 min — check the predicted time at the visitor center). Then walk the Upper Geyser Basin boardwalk, which has the highest concentration of geysers on Earth. Castle and Grand geysers to kill a little time, or all the way to Morning Glory if we want to use up more than an hour.

title: Little Gibbon Falls
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 1.6 mi out-and-back, easy. A scenic trail leading to the base of Gibbon Falls, offering beautiful views of the surrounding landscape.

title: Norris Geyser Basin
description: 🌋 Geologic Feature: The hottest and most dynamic thermal basin in the park. Steamboat Geyser is the world's tallest active geyser. Walk the Back Basin and Porcelain Basin loops (combined ~2.5 mi, easy boardwalks) to see boiling pools, fumaroles, and Echinus Geyser.

title: Artist Paint Pots Trail
description: 🥾 Short Hike: 0.9 mi loop, easy. A fun, accessible trail winding through colorful mud pots and small geysers. The bubbling pink and gray mud pots are a highlight — great for kids.

title: Grand Prismatic Spring & Fairy Falls
description: Alert: Trail might be closed due to bear attack. 🌋 Geologic Feature + 🥾 Hike: Grand Prismatic Spring is Yellowstone's largest hot spring and most photographed thermal feature. Start with the Fairy Falls Trail (4.9 mi out-and-back, easy) — the overlook at mile 1 gives the iconic aerial view of the rainbow-colored spring. Continue to Fairy Falls itself, a stunning 200-ft waterfall at the end of the trail.

title: Lone Star Geyser Trail
description: 🥾 Hike + ⛲ Geyser: 5.3 mi out-and-back, easy. Follows the Firehole River through a peaceful backcountry corridor to a cone geyser that erupts every ~3 hours with a 30-50 ft column. Check the logbook at the trailhead for the last eruption time to time your arrival. It'll take about an hour to get there. One of the most rewarding uncrowded experiences in the park.

title: Spin Fishing at Lewis Lake
description: 🎣 Lure Fishing: Lewis Lake is renowned for excellent shore lure fishing. Cast lead-free, barbless spinners (like Panther Martins) or spoons (like Jake's Spin-A-Lure) from the shore near the lake inlet/outlet or pullouts along the South Entrance Road. Good chance of catching Lake Trout or Brown Trout.

details.Gear: 2 Lures (Panther Martin/Jake's Spin-A-Lure), 6 lb fluorocarbon leader
details.Regulations: Barbless hooks required; lead-free bait only

title: Bradley Lake Trail
description: 🥾 Moderate Hike: 5.3 mi loop, 565 ft elevation gain. A scenic and relatively gentle trail that loops past the forested shores of both Taggart Lake and Bradley Lake. Resting directly beneath the massive peaks of the Teton Range, this hike offers outstanding mountain reflections and beautiful aspen groves.

title: Cottonwood Creek
description: 🏞️ Scenic Walk & Picnic: A quiet, beautiful spot near Moose along the Teton Park Road where Cottonwood Creek flows. Walk down to the water, enjoy a picnic, and view the nearby historic Jimmy Manges Cabin, the oldest standing homestead structure in Jackson Hole.

title: Delta Lake Loop
description: 🥾 Strenuous Scramble: 8.0 mi round-trip, 2,300 ft elevation gain. An unofficial, unmarked, and physically grueling trail starting from Lupine Meadows. The final 3/4 mile requires scrambling over steep boulder fields and dirt slopes, but rewards you with an up-close, breathtaking view of the brilliant turquoise Delta Lake sitting directly underneath the Grand Teton.

details.Shoe Recommendation: Hiking Shoes / Boots (Sturdy support & good traction required)

title: Drive to the top of Signal Mountain
address: Signal Mountain Summit Road, Moran, WY 83013
description: 🚗 Scenic summit drive: Follow the winding road up Signal Mountain for one of the broadest panorama views in Grand Teton National Park. From the summit overlook you can see Jackson Lake, the Teton Range, the Snake River valley, and much of the park laid out below you with very little walking required.

details.Highlights: Jackson Lake, Teton skyline, Snake River overlook
details.Road Notes: Paved mountain road with a short walk from the parking area to viewpoints

title: Hidden Falls, Inspiration Point, Cascade Canyon Trail
description: 🥾 Challenging Hike: 9.3 mi out-and-back, 1,100 ft elevation gain. One of the most famous and scenic hikes in the Rocky Mountains. Take the Jenny Lake shuttle boat across the water, then ascend past Hidden Falls and Inspiration Point into the breathtaking, steep-walled Cascade Canyon. High chance of spotting moose in the willows.

details.Highlights: Hidden Falls, Inspiration Point, Canyon View

title: Schwabacher Trail
description: 📷 Scenic Walk: 1.3 mi round-trip, flat. A world-famous photography spot along the Snake River. Beaver dams create perfectly still pools that reflect the Grand Teton. Excellent for a morning walk and highly active for wildlife like moose and waterfowl.

title: Drive through Mormon Row
address: Mormon Row Historic District, Antelope Flats Rd, Moose, WY 83012
description: 📷 Scenic drive and photo stop: Cruise the quiet road through the historic Mormon Row district for iconic barn views with the Tetons rising behind them. This is one of the best sunrise or morning photography spots in the park, and it's also a good place to watch for bison and pronghorn in the surrounding sage flats.

details.Highlights: Moulton Barns, open Teton views, wildlife in Antelope Flats
details.Road Notes: Mostly flat gravel road with easy pull-offs for quick stops

title: Bike ride
description: 🥾 Bike Ride: Pick up bikes in Jackson. We can probably ride to the Big Rocks Trail and Black Canyon Loop and then to Phelps Lake and back.

title: Big Rocks Trail and Black Canyon Loop
description: 🥾 Hike: Explore this 2.7-mile loop trail near Wilson, Wyoming. Generally considered a moderately challenging route, it takes an average of 1 h 26 min to complete. This is a popular trail for hiking, horseback riding, and trail running, but you can still enjoy some solitude during quieter times of day.

title: Lakeshore Trail
description: 🥾 Easy Hike: 2.2 mi loop, 100 ft elevation gain. Located at Colter Bay. A flat, paved figure-eight loop that follows the forested shoreline of Jackson Lake. Offers spectacular, easy-access views of the lake and the Teton Range, including Mount Moran — perfect for a relaxing stroll.

title: Snake River scenic float trip
address: Snake River, Grand Teton National Park, WY 83012
description: 🛶 Scenic float: A relaxed guided float down the Snake River with big Teton views and excellent wildlife-watching potential. Expect chances to spot bald eagles, osprey, pelicans, beavers, and sometimes moose along the banks while drifting through one of the park's most photogenic corridors.

details.Best Time: Morning for calmer water and wildlife activity
details.Highlights: Teton views from the river, birdlife, beaver lodges, mellow sightseeing

title: Colter Bay Hermitage Point Trail
description: 🥾 Moderate Hike: 9.7 mi loop, 930 ft elevation gain. A long but relatively flat, rolling hike exploring the peninsula extending into Jackson Lake from Colter Bay. Winds through lodgepole pine forests, meadows, Swan Lake, and Heron Pond. Perfect for viewing trumpeter swans, moose, and waterfowl before reaching the scenic point.
